title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
What is the Right time to Buy an Annuity plan?
Annuity plans are among the most effective and popular insurance products for retirement planning. It provides a stable and regular income post-retirement. However, the optimum impact of your planning depends on the timing of your purchase. Knowing the best time to buy an annuity can unlock greater returns and long-term security. Intrigued? Keep reading to discover how choosing the right time to buy an annuity plan can set you up for a worry-free financial future.
What are Annuity Plans?
Before we get to the main part of the article, let’s quickly touch upon the concept of annuity plans.
The word ‘annuity’ here refers to a string of payments made at regular intervals. In the context of life insurance, an annuity plan is a contract between an individual and an insurance service provider and the annuity is guaranteed for the period mentioned in the policy, depending upon the option chosen.
Here, the individual agrees to make either a lump sum payment or multiple smaller payments for a specific period of time to the insurance service provider. And in return, the insurance provider agrees to pay a steady stream of income regularly to the individual, starting either immediately or at a later point in time as per the plan’s terms & conditions.
Annuity plans provide a steady stream of income to the individual till the time of their demise. There are many options in annuity, and depending on the option chosen, either a lump sum amount or periodic income is paid out. And upon the death of the individual, the insurance provider shall return all or part of the purchase price to the nominee listed under the annuity plan, based on the terms and conditions of the annuity option chosen.
What are the Types of Annuity?
Immediate and deferred annuities are the two types of annuities that you can choose from.
1. Immediate annuity
An immediate annuity begins paying out shortly after you make a lump-sum payment to the life insurance provider, making it ideal if you want a predictable income stream. Typically, the best time to buy this type of annuity is when you have accumulated sufficient savings. It allows you to choose your payout frequency—monthly, quarterly, half-yearly, or annually—based on your financial needs and retirement goals.
2. Deferred annuity
In a deferred annuity plan, on the other hand, the annuity payments don’t start immediately after you pay a lump sum amount. Instead, you can choose to receive the annuity payments at a later point in time. There are two distinct phases in a deferred annuity plan - the accumulation phase and the income phase.
During the accumulation phase, you either pay a lump sum amount or a series of smaller payments to the insurance provider or wait for your corpus to grow. And during the income phase, you begin to start receiving regular annuity pay-outs.
Annuity options
When you purchase an annuity plan, there are different annuity options that you can choose from. Let’s take a quick look at some of the common ones. Annuity options may vary from one plan to another.
1. Life annuity
In a life annuity plan, the annuity payments continue to be disbursed to the individual till the time of their demise. And upon their demise, the annuity of payments will stop from thereon.
2. Life annuity with return of purchase price
In a life annuity with return of purchase price, the annuitant receives a regular income for as long as they live. After the annuitant’s death, the insurer returns 100% of the purchase price to the nominee. This option applies to a single annuitant unless specifically chosen under a joint-life variant.
Note: However, financial planning is unique for individuals, the ideal time to buy this type of annuity plan is when you are nearing retirement and want to convert your accumulated savings into a lifelong income.
3. Joint life annuity
A joint life annuity plan is basically a plan that covers two individuals, i.e. spouses. Here, the annuity payments will continue to be made until either you or your spouse remains alive. Upon the death of both individuals, the annuity payments would cease.
4. Joint life annuity with return of purchase price
This is very similar to a joint life annuity plan, but with an extra feature of return of purchase price. Here, the annuity payments are made until either of the spouses remains alive. Upon the death of both spouses, the annuity payments would cease, and the entire purchase price would be returned to the nominee listed under the annuity plan.
Benefits of Purchasing Annuities at Various Ages
Choosing when to invest in an annuity can significantly impact your financial management, particularly after retirement. Different stages of life offer distinct considerations and opportunities to maximize benefits.
In Your 20s-30s
When you are in your 20s or 30s, you probably have lesser responsibilities or financial obligations. Thus, you can plan your retirement without stress. When you buy an annuity plan during this phase, the capital is locked which ultimately will help you in your post-retirement life. In a nutshell, by buying annuity plans early can help you plan your retirement smoothly without any financial stress.
In Your 40s-50s
This stage of life is generally suitable for deferred annuities because the premiums you pay are invested by the insurer in a mix of asset classes, such as government securities, corporate bonds, and sometimes equities, depending on the product. These investments are allowed to grow during the deferment period, helping you accumulate a larger retirement corpus. Since payouts start only after this accumulation phase, you can align them with your retirement age for a steady post-retirement income. However, early withdrawals may lead to penalties or reduced benefits, so choosing the appropriate deferment period and annuity option becomes essential.
In Your 50s-60s
Many people find the later phase of their career them best age to buy an annuity.  This is what helps you benefit from higher payout rates, because annuity rates typically increase with age; the same purchase price can secure a higher monthly pension when you buy the annuity later in life. You can choose between immediate and deferred annuity plans to supplement your retirement income while optimizing returns without locking in capital for decades.
65+ and Beyond
At this age, immediate annuities tend to offer higher payout amounts because the insurer expects to pay the income for a shorter period. While the overall duration of payouts is limited due to age, this option ensures a stable and guaranteed monthly income, providing financial security without depending heavily on other investments.1
How to Buy an Annuity Plan
Buying an annuity is a key step in securing a steady retirement income. Choosing the best age to buy an annuity can help you maximize payouts and match it with your financial goals.
Assess Your Retirement Goals
Start by estimating your retirement expenses, current savings, and other income sources. This helps determine whether you need immediate income or can defer payouts .
Determine Your Investment Capacity
Decide if you can invest a lump sum or prefer periodic contributions. Your financial position influences the type of annuity and the payout schedule. Online calculators can simulate potential returns based on age and investment.
Consider the Optimal Age
The right time to buy an annuity does not depend on a specific age range. It largely depends on when you plan to retire and the type of payout you want. If you want income to start right away, an immediate annuity is suitable. If you prefer income to begin later, a deferred annuity allows you to start payouts after a chosen period. Selecting the right option ensures your annuity aligns with your retirement timeline and income needs.2
Consult a Financial Advisor
A certified advisor can help evaluate tax implications, payout frequency, and joint-life options. Personalized guidance can help you choose an annuity plan, aligned with your retirement strategy.
Complete the Application
After selecting a plan, submit your KYC documents, choose your preferred payment mode, and confirm your payout preferences. Investing at the right age will secure a predictable income and reduce financial uncertainty in retirement.
Key Takeaways
There is no best time to buy an annuity. It largely depends on when you plan to retire and the type of payout you want.
Choosing the right annuity type, immediate or deferred, at the optimal age will provide you with a predictable post-retirement income stream.
Conclusion
The best time to buy an annuity depends on various factors discussed in the blog. Furthermore, you must choose the appropriate annuity type and payment option to ensure financial stability, and plan early to take advantage of reinvestment opportunities to further grow your wealth. These steps can make retirement planning precise and predictable.
FAQs
When to buy an Annuity Plan?
There is no fixed or ideal age to purchase an annuity plan. It depends on your retirement plan majorly. However, renowned insurance companies suggest customers of age 45 years and above should typically purchase annuity plans.
Can a person buy an annuity at age 75?
Individuals can definitely purchase an annuity plan at age 75, provided they meet the annuity plan’s age and health requirements. However, the premium amount may be higher.
What is the average age of annuity buyers?
Although young people can also buy an annuity plan, the average age of annuity buyers typically falls between 50 and 55 years. The reason behind this is that this age range aligns well with retirement planning. Further, it offers better annuity rates while allowing investors to secure a steady income stream closer to retirement.
Are there age-specific limitations for purchasing an annuity?
Age restrictions vary across insurers.  For the Bajaj Life Insurance Guaranteed Pension Goal II plan: Minimum entry age is 30 years for an immediate annuity and 35 years for a deferred annuity. Maximum entry age is 85 years (immediate) and 84 years (deferred).
